      <title>Joel Embiid To Undergo Appendectomy</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285016/Joel-Embiid-To-Undergo-Appendectomy</link>
      <description><p>Joel Embiid has been diagnosed with appendicitis and will undergo surgery this afternoon in Houston.</p>
<p>Embiid has appeared in 38 games this season, averaging 26.9 points, 7.7 rebounds and 3.9 assists in 31.6 minutes. Embiid has a PER of 24.5.</p>
<p>NBA players have roughly needed three weeks to recover from an appendectomy before returning to game action.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 15:32:28 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285016/Joel-Embiid-To-Undergo-Appendectomy</guid>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>AJ Dybantsa Moves To No. 1 In ESPN's 2026 NBA Draft Big Board, Top Of Class Remains Open</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285013/AJ-Dybantsa-Moves-To-No-1-In-ESPNs-2026-NBA-Draft-Big-Board-Top-Of-Class-Remains-Open</link>
      <description><p>AJ Dybantsa has claimed the top position in ESPN's updated 2026 NBA Draft rankings, displacing Darryn Peterson atop the big board following a strong close to his freshman season at BYU that solidified his standing among league executives.</p>
<p>Dybantsa, a 6-foot-9 wing, averaged 25.5 points, 6.8 rebounds and 3.7 assists across 35 games, demonstrating an ability to carry a significant offensive workload while showing growth as a decision-maker. </p>
<p>"Dybantsa has become the player NBA executives most commonly project to be selected first, with the expectation that he'll be a star wing scorer with the capacity to drive winning on both sides of the ball in the long run," <a href="https://www.espn.com/nba/story/_/id/46886245/2026-nba-draft-big-board-rankings-top-100-prospects-players">wrote Jeremy Woo</a>. "In a draft replete with high-level talent, he has positioned himself as the easiest answer at No. 1."</p>
<p>Despite ceding ground in the No. 1 conversation, Peterson remains a compelling candidate. Scouts view the Kansas freshman as the most gifted scorer and shotmaker in the class, though a full-body cramping episode in the preseason clouded his freshman year both physically and mentally. His medical report and predraft interviews will carry significant weight as teams assess his long-term availability.</p>
<p>Duke's Cameron Boozer holds the third position after <a href="shman-To-Win-AP-Player-Of-The-Year">a dominant season that included Naismith and AP Player of the Year honors</a>. Boozer led college basketball with a 17.1 box plus-minus score and drew comparisons from scouts to Domantas Sabonis and Kevin Love, though questions persist about his defensive ceiling relative to the other top options.</p>
<p>North Carolina's Caleb Wilson sits fourth despite missing the postseason with hand injuries, while Illinois guard Keaton Wagler cemented his lottery standing with a Final Four run. Arkansas point guard Darius Acuff Jr. vaulted to sixth after winning SEC Player of the Year and leading the Razorbacks to a Sweet 16 berth.</p>
<p>One significant storyline heading into the predraft process is roster attrition. NIL earnings and transfer portal flexibility continue to make returning to college an appealing financial decision for many underclassmen, creating uncertainty around the depth of the available pool beyond the lottery. The NCAA's early entry withdrawal deadline is May 27.</p>
<p>The draft combine and lottery are scheduled for May 10, with the draft itself expected in late June. The past two <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/lottery_winners">NBA draft lottery winners</a> had a three percent chance or worse to win the lottery, while the previous four seasons were won by teams that finished in the bottom-3 with 14 percent odds.</p>

      <title>Giannis Antetokounmpo Ruled Out For Bucks' Home Finale On Friday</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285019/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Ruled-Out-For-Bucks-Home-Finale-On-Friday</link>
      <description><p>Giannis Antetokounmpo has been ruled out by the Milwaukee Bucks for their regular season finale on Friday against the Brooklyn Nets. Antetokounmpo has been out since suffered a hyperextended left knee and a bone bruise on March 15th.</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo is widely expected to be traded this offseason if he doesn't signal his intention to sign a five-year, $275 million extension in October with the Bucks.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284953/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Lost-Nike-Bonus-Tied-To-Games-Played-After-Bucks-Refused-To-Clear-Him">The relationship between Antetokounmpo and the Bucks turned contentious in recent weeks</a> over their decision not to clear him to return to game action. </p>
<p>Since winning back-to-back NBA Most Valuable Player awards in 2019 and 2020, Antetokounmpo has finished fourth, third, third, fourth and third over the past five years. Antetokounmpo was in the MVP conversation early in the season before he suffered multiple calf strains. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:36:18 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285019/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Ruled-Out-For-Bucks-Home-Finale-On-Friday</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_250410.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Tyrese Haliburton Plays Five-On-Five For First Time In Rehab Process</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285008/Tyrese-Haliburton-Plays-Five-On-Five-For-First-Time-In-Rehab-Process</link>
      <description><p>On Wednesday, Indiana Pacers guard Tyrese Haliburton shared on social media that he played five-on-five for the first time as part of his recovery from a torn Achilles' tendon. Haliburton is in week 41 of his recovery since tearing his Achilles' in the 2025 NBA Finals.</p>
<p>This is a milestone moment for Haliburton, as returning to full court play is a major step in the rehab process. The Pacers and their star guard have both expressed that they expect him to be a full participant in training camp this fall.</p>
<p>For reference, Boston Celtics forward Jayson Tatum returned to play in roughly 43 weeks. For Haliburton, with the Pacers season coming to an end this weekend, the next time we'll see him play for Indiana will be in the 2026 NBA preseason.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 09:04:23 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285008/Tyrese-Haliburton-Plays-Five-On-Five-For-First-Time-In-Rehab-Process</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Haliburton_Tyrese_ind_250601b.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Stanford's Ebuka Okorie Declares For NBA Draft After Record Freshman Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285021/Stanfords-Ebuka-Okorie-Declares-For-NBA-Draft-After-Record-Freshman-Season</link>
      <description><p>Stanford guard Ebuka Okorie announced Thursday he will enter the NBA Draft following a record-breaking freshman season in which he led the ACC in scoring and established himself as one of the top prospects in the country.</p>
<p>Okorie, who arrived at Stanford with no high-major scholarship offers out of New Hampshire, averaged 23.2 points per game to finish eighth nationally in scoring. The only freshmen ahead of him were projected lottery picks AJ Dybantsa of BYU and Darius Acuff Jr. of Arkansas.</p>
<p>"A year ago you guys took a chance on a kid from New Hampshire with zero high major offers, welcoming me with open arms and allowing me to be the best version of myself on and off the court," Okorie said in a social media post thanking coach Kyle Smith and the Stanford staff.</p>
<p>Okorie made an immediate impact, scoring 26 points in his debut against Portland State before breaking out with a 36-point effort in an upset win over North Carolina in January. He scored 40 points in a home victory over Georgia Tech and finished with eight 30-point games on the season, breaking the ACC freshman record previously held by Duke's Marvin Bagley III.</p>
<p>His 719 total points rank third in Stanford history for a single season, trailing only Adam Keefe's 734 in 1991-92 and Chasson Randle's 724 in 2014-15.</p>
<p>Okorie earned first-team All-ACC honors and honorable mention AP All-American recognition, adding 3.6 rebounds, 3.6 assists and 1.6 steals per game. The last player to reach those combined per-game marks was Ja Morant at Murray State in 2018-19.</p>
<p>His perimeter shooting also improved significantly as the season progressed, connecting on 46.9% of his three-point attempts over his final 12 games. He is projected as a late first-round selection.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:45:47 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285021/Stanfords-Ebuka-Okorie-Declares-For-NBA-Draft-After-Record-Freshman-Season</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Stanford.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jaylen Brown Turned Celtics' Gap-Year Season Into 'Opportunity To Show World What I Could Do'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285014/Jaylen-Brown-Turned-Celtics-Gap-Year-Season-Into-Opportunity-To-Show-World-What-I-Could-Do</link>
      <description><p>When the Boston Celtics lost three starters from their championship roster and Jayson Tatum ruptured his Achilles tendon, the league largely wrote off the 2025-26 season as a transitional year, but Jaylen Brown had a different interpretation entirely.</p>
<p>"From a financial standpoint, this was a rebuild, right?" Brown told ESPN's Ramona Shelburne. "But I didn't look at it like that. I looked at it as an opportunity to show the world who I am and what I could do."</p>
<p>Brown has done exactly that as is posting career highs in points, rebounds and assists, carries the second-highest usage rate in the NBA and has guided Boston to the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference, a level of success almost no one predicted when the season began.</p>
<p>The foundation for what Brown has built this season was laid in isolation last summer. Three weeks after undergoing surgery to repair a torn meniscus, he was alone in his Boston home, turning away calls from friends, teammates and family. He did not want comfort. He wanted space.</p>
<p>"One of my toxic traits is that I have a hard time letting people see me weak," Brown said.</p>
<p>During that rehab, Brown meditated and studied his teammates' astrological charts and numerology in an effort to customize his approach to each personality in the locker room. Brown also did red light therapy on his knee several times daily.</p>
<p>Brad Stevens reminded Brown that the franchise had <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Boston-Celtics/2/roster-turnover">rebuilt its roster several times</a> since drafting him third overall in 2017, and that Boston still expected to compete despite everything that had changed.</p>
<p>"A lot of guys would have misconstrued that and not done what he's done," Stevens told ESPN. "And what he's done is he's played great, and he's empowered others. We needed him to do both for our team to be really good."</p>
<p>Stevens told Brown that the key was recognizing what the new pieces around him were capable of.</p>
<p>"The only thing that a lot of these guys were, was unproven," Stevens said. "I think Brown knew Jordan Walsh could play. That Baylor Scheierman could play. That Neemias Queta and Luka Garza could play. But he also knew that by showing belief in them, he would get a lot out of them."</p>
<p>Brown took that charge seriously. He hosted team dinners. He mentored younger players individually. He advocated publicly for Queta's Most Improved Player and All-Defensive Team candidacy. Walsh, who calls Brown "Unc" because of the mentorship they've built, became one of several teammates to elevate alongside him.</p>
<p>Boston's numbers reflect the collaborative nature of Brown's influence. The Celtics post an effective field goal percentage of 65.2% off passes from Brown, fifth highest among players with 500 or more assists this season. Teammates Payton Pritchard, Sam Hauser, Derrick White and Queta are all averaging career highs in scoring.</p>
<p>"Jaylen has believed in this group from the get-go, and that includes the summer and that includes when we were 0-3," Stevens said. "I think that's helped bring out the best in all of those guys. He has seemed to find a lot of joy in helping them prove themselves."</p>
<p>For much of his career, Brown's edge came from perceived slights, trade rumors and a sense that the broader basketball world undervalued him. This season, something has shifted. </p>
<p>"At times, I think I would make myself small, for other people to feel comfortable," Brown said. "There's a difference between that and making yourself small and dimming your light."</p>
<p>Tatum returned from his Achilles injury in March and has watched up close what Brown built in his absence. He has no doubt about what drove it.</p>
<p>"Obviously he's somebody that's always been capable," Tatum told ESPN. "This was just an opportunity where more was required from everybody, but especially him. The NBA is all about opportunity and the guys who really make the most out of it. The special ones do, and that's exactly what he's been able to do this year."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 12:48:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285014/Jaylen-Brown-Turned-Celtics-Gap-Year-Season-Into-Opportunity-To-Show-World-What-I-Could-Do</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brown_Jaylen_bos_250504.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Clears Kings, Says Late-Game Foul On Seth Curry Was Coaching Error, Not Tanking</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285020/NBA-Clears-Kings-Says-Late-Game-Foul-On-Seth-Curry-Was-Coaching-Error-Not-Tanking</link>
      <description><p>The NBA has cleared Sacramento Kings coach Doug Christie of tanking following an investigation into his decision to foul Golden State Warriors guard Seth Curry in the final minutes of Tuesday's 110-105 loss, determining the move was a tactical miscalculation rather than a deliberate effort to lose.</p>
<p>"The investigation found that Christie made no intentional effort to give the Warriors a shooting foul, or to cause the Kings to lose the game," the league said in a statement.</p>
<p>The foul, ordered with 3:15 remaining and the Kings trailing by one, sent Curry to the free-throw line and set off a social media firestorm accusing Sacramento of blatant tanking. Draymond Green amplified the controversy after the game, citing the decision as further evidence of the league's broader competitive integrity problem.</p>
<p>Christie and Kings officials maintained from the outset that the foul was a straightforward coaching mistake. Christie intended to preserve a timeout before automatically losing it when the clock dropped below three minutes, planning to draw up a play on the subsequent possession. He was unaware the Kings were already in the bonus, which sent Curry to the line rather than granting a side-out inbounds.</p>
<p>The strategy behind the foul ultimately worked. Christie drew up a play for Doug McDermott out of the timeout that resulted in a corner three-pointer, putting Sacramento ahead by three with 2:53 remaining. The Kings ultimately surrendered that lead in the closing minutes.</p>
<p>The broader evidence also supports Christie's account. Since sitting at 12-46 on Feb. 21, Sacramento has gone 9-13, a stretch inconsistent with a team actively pursuing losses. Christie has been direct on the subject.</p>
<p>"Tanking is the last thing I'd do," Christie said after a March 15 win over Utah. "I respect the game too much. These young men, when you do things like that, it hurts them."</p>
<p>"If we wanted to tank, we're doing an awful job of it," said one source with the Kings.</p>
<p>The episode arrives as the NBA continues to struggle with the broader tanking dilemma. The Utah Jazz and Indiana Pacers were fined $500,000 and $100,000 respectively in February for violating the league's player participation policy. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284708/NBA-Presented-Three-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Options-To-Board-Of-Governors">Commissioner Adam Silver has signaled significant lottery reform is coming for next season</a>, though those measures have yet to take effect.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:40:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285020/NBA-Clears-Kings-Says-Late-Game-Foul-On-Seth-Curry-Was-Coaching-Error-Not-Tanking</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Christie_Doug_sac_241227.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Magic Fined $25,000 Over Anthony Black Injury Reporting</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285015/Magic-Fined-$25000-Over-Anthony-Black-Injury-Reporting</link>
      <description><p>The NBA announced today that the Orlando Magic organization has been fined $25,000 for violating league injury reporting rules. </p>
<p>The Magic failed to accurately disclose the game availability status of Anthony Black prior to their game against the Detroit Pistons on April 6. Black was listed as "Out" in Orlando's initial injury report and subsequently played in the game. </p>
<p>Black played 15 minutes and scored 14 points in the game.</p></description>

      <description><p style="position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; text-align: center; z-index: 9999; margin: 0; padding: 10px; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);"><a href="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 0;" type="button" value="STREAM THUNDER VS NUGGETS ON FUBO TODAY!" /> </a></p>
<p style="position: fixed; top: 0px; left: 0px; width: 100%; text-align: center; z-index: 9999; margin: 0px; padding-top: 10px; padding-right: 10px; padding-bottom: 10px; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);"> </p>
<p>The NBA regular season reaches a fever pitch this Friday, April 10, as the Oklahoma City Thunder travel to the Mile High City to face the Denver Nuggets at Ball Arena. Oklahoma City (63-16) enters the night as the undisputed top seed in the Western Conference, riding a multi-game winning streak and looking to sweep the season series against their rivals. </p>
<p><a href="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> <input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 10px;" type="button" value="WATCH THE THUNDER VS NUGGETS" /> </a></p>
<div id="fuboWidgetContain" style="max-width: 640px;">
<div class="fuboWidget" style="position: relative; padding-top: 66.05%;"><iframe class="video" style="position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%;" src="https://www.fubo.tv/stream/nba/?irad=1846663&irmp=3589123&subId1=RealGM" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" allowfullscreen="allowfullscreen"></iframe></div>
</div>
<p>On the other side, the Nuggets (51-28) are in a dogfight for the second seed, currently tied with San Antonio in the loss column. Denver is coming off a dominant victory over Memphis and is looking to protect home court to secure a more favorable path through the Western Conference playoffs. With the post-season just days away, tonight's matchup serves as a potential Conference Finals preview. Catch the action live on Fubo. </p>
<p>The star power in this matchup is unrivaled. The Thunder are led by MVP frontrunner <strong>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander</strong>-averaging a historic 31.3 PPG-and the defensive mastery of <strong>Chet Holmgren</strong>. Denver counters with the triple-double machine <strong>Nikola Jokić</strong> and the elite shot-making of <strong>Jamal Murray</strong>. In their last meeting in March, OKC narrowly escaped with a 129-126 victory, making tonight's rematch personal for the home crowd.</p>
<p>Denver's elite home record will be tested by an Oklahoma City squad that has proven to be the most efficient road team in the league this season. You can witness this Western Conference heavyweight clash streaming live on Fubo.</p>

      <title>Jackie Young Becomes First WNBA Player To Sign Million Dollar Contract In Return To Aces</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285022/Jackie-Young-Becomes-First-WNBA-Player-To-Sign-Million-Dollar-Contract-In-Return-To-Aces</link>
      <description><p>Four-time All-Star guard Jackie Young is finalizing a one-year agreement to return to the Las Vegas Aces worth $1.19 million, sources told ESPN Thursday, making her the first player in WNBA history to sign a million dollar contract.</p>
<p>Young did not take meetings with any other teams despite being one of the most sought-after free agents this offseason. Her return keeps the Aces' core intact alongside four-time MVP A'ja Wilson, who has already stated her intent to remain in Las Vegas, and six-time All-Star Chelsea Gray.</p>
<p>The agreement preserves the franchise that has won three championships in four years, including the 2025 title.</p>
<p>Young, 27, has spent her entire professional career with Las Vegas after being selected first overall in the 2019 draft out of Notre Dame. She earned Most Improved Player honors in 2022 and has been named to four All-Star teams and two All-WNBA squads. In 2025 she averaged 16.5 points, 5.1 assists, 4.5 rebounds and 1.3 steals per game.</p>
<p>The 6-foot guard is also a two-time Olympic gold medalist, winning with the U.S. 3-on-3 team in Tokyo and the 5-on-5 team in Paris. She won a national championship at Notre Dame in 2018.</p>
<p>Young's contract marks a landmark moment for the WNBA as <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284484/New-WNBA-CBA-Features-$600K-Average-Salary-$7M-Salary-Cap-20-perc-Revenue-Sharing">the league enters a new era following its most recent collective bargaining agreement</a>, one designed to substantially increase player compensation at the top of the market.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 22:01:31 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285022/Jackie-Young-Becomes-First-WNBA-Player-To-Sign-Million-Dollar-Contract-In-Return-To-Aces</guid>

   </item>

      <title>Nickeil Alexander-Walker Says Quin Snyder Saw Higher Ceiling For Him Than He Saw For Himself</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285007/Nickeil-Alexander-Walker-Says-Quin-Snyder-Saw-Higher-Ceiling-For-Him-Than-He-Saw-For-Himself</link>
      <description><p>Nickeil Alexander-Walker credits Quin Snyder with unlocking a version of himself with the Atlanta Hawks he had never been asked to find, describing a belief from his coach that exceeded his own self-assessment heading into this season. The emergence has Alexander-Walker as one of the favorites to win <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/Most-Improved-Player/4">Most Improved Player</a>.</p>
<p>"He saw a ceiling higher for myself than I did," Alexander-Walker told NBA.com. "And that was really cool for me because I felt like in my career I've never had that before."</p>
<p>Snyder's vision was specific, seeing Alexander-Walker as a player capable of averaging 20 points, contributing as a primary playmaker and sustaining the defensive impact that made him an attractive free agent target last summer. The guard, who signed with Atlanta after helping the Minnesota Timberwolves reach back-to-back Western Conference Finals, had spent his career in a defined role.</p>
<p>"He sees me as a person who can do more than what I was doing," Alexander-Walker said. "And that's something that was very important to me: Could they see the potential through what I was only able to show them through my role?"</p>
<p>With the Timberwolves, his responsibilities were straightforward by his own description.</p>
<p>"Corner 3, get to the paint, eyes out, defend," said Alexander-Walker. </p>
<p>Snyder pushed him further, specifically challenging Alexander-Walker to take contested shots without hesitation.</p>
<p>"He was like, 'Hey, there might come a time throughout the game we might need you to take a contested shot,'" Alexander-Walker said. "A contested shot can be deemed a bad shot, but not really, because a lot of great players in this league very rarely are they getting uncontested looks."</p>
<p>Even after a sluggish start, Snyder's message never wavered.</p>
<p>"Because our hearts are in the right place, they often align with how we think and act," Alexander-Walker said. "And that makes it easier for him and I to work together to find my spots and settle into being a decision-maker."</p>
<p>Snyder said the foundation of their approach was simple and consistent.</p>
<p>"The thing that we've tried to impress upon him from the outset is that we need his aggressiveness and for him to understand that that's not tied to whether or not the shot goes in," Snyder said. "He's got such a tremendous work ethic, it's logical that he's going to improve as the season goes on."</p>
<p>Alexander-Walker called this season "the most fun I've had playing basketball."</p>
<p>"I've worked so hard for something like this," he said. "Nourish it. Treat it correctly. Honor it. Value it. That's my focus."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 09 Apr 2026 07:29:40 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Alexanderwalker_Nickeil_atl_251103.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Thunder Secure No. 1 Seed, Home Court Advantage For Playoffs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285006/Thunder-Secure-No-1-Seed-Home-Court-Advantage-For-Playoffs</link>
      <description><p>The Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Los Angeles Clippers 128-110 on Wednesday night to clinch the best record in the NBA.</p>
<p>The Thunder also secured home court advantage for the playoffs with the win.</p>
<p>"Now obviously we go to the playoffs and it's a blank slate," Thunder coach Mark Daigneault said. "This doesn't carry over, but it is good to have home court. And it's good to have a little bit of momentum from the season."</p>
<p>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander finished with 20 points and 11 assists, with Jalen Williams adding 18 points.</p>
<p>Gilgeous-Alexander has scored at least 20 points in 141 straight games. </p>
<p>The Thunder currently have the fourth-most wins over a two-year span with 132. </p>
<p>The team also secured the No. 1 seed in the West for the third straight season. This feat has only been accomplished by the 19984-88 Boston Celtics, 1984-90 Los Angeles Lakers, 1996-98 Chicago Bulls, 2008-10 Lakers and 2015-17 Golden State Warriors. All of those teams won multiple NBA championships during that run.</p>
<p>"It's impressive," said Gilgeous-Alexander. "Obviously, none of it means anything if you don't win in the end, and you know that. But teams that win in the end have some of the same characteristics and traits and check off the same boxes.</p>
<p>"So checking off those boxes is kind of just like knowing we're headed in the right direction. Obviously, we're far from the finish line of where we want to go, but like I said, it lets us know we're heading right there."</p></description>

      <title>Zaccharie Risacher Records First Career DNP As Hawks Tighten Rotation Amid Playoff Push</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285002/Zaccharie-Risacher-Records-First-Career-DNP-As-Hawks-Tighten-Rotation-Amid-Playoff-Push</link>
      <description><p>Zaccharie Risacher, the first overall pick in the 2024 NBA Draft, was a healthy scratch for the first time in his career Wednesday as the Atlanta Hawks fell 122-116 to the Cleveland Cavaliers, spending his 21st birthday on the bench for the duration.</p>
<p>The development marks a significant shift for the young French forward, who started the first two-thirds of the season before losing his spot in the lineup following a 128-97 home loss to the Miami Heat that dropped Atlanta to 27-31. Since CJ McCollum replaced Risacher in the starting five, the Hawks have gone 18-4 and climbed to fifth in the Eastern Conference.</p>
<p>The arrival of Jonathan Kuminga further complicated Risacher's situation. The two players occupy similar positional roles, and with Kuminga now at full strength following a knee injury, Atlanta rarely deploys both simultaneously. Risacher played a career-low seven minutes in Monday's loss to New York before being ruled out entirely Wednesday, with Corey Kispert inserted into the rotation ahead of him.</p>
<p>Quin Snyder has been careful to frame Risacher's diminished role as circumstantial rather than performance-based.</p>
<p>"I think Zach's been doing all the things that I've talked about, that we've asked," Snyder said. "We only have so many guys that we can actually put in the game. JK played a little more, so those minutes come from somewhere. It's not anything about one player. That's just the game."</p>
<p>Snyder added: "We need him to keep doing the things that he's been doing. I mean, he's great. He's been crashing the glass. Those are big plays for us."</p>
<p>Atlanta's two most recent opponents, Cleveland and the New York Knicks, are also the franchise's most probable first-round playoff foes. With the rotation tightening around that reality, a near-term path back to consistent minutes for Risacher appears narrow.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Michael Malone Initially Turned Down North Carolina With Focus On NBA Return</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285001/Michael-Malone-Initially-Turned-Down-North-Carolina-With-Focus-On-NBA-Return</link>
      <description><p>Michael Malone repeatedly declined North Carolina's overtures before agreeing to become the program's head coach, revealing Tuesday that he initially assumed the Tar Heels' contact was little more than a formality.</p>
<p>When incoming athletic director Steve Newmark first reached out, Malone told him college basketball was not a serious consideration. He was focused on returning to the NBA following his dismissal from the Denver Nuggets and viewed the UNC inquiry as a courtesy call rather than a genuine pursuit.</p>
<p>"Every time I said no, I was regretting that," Malone said at his introductory press conference. "I thought, I think I'm making a mistake."</p>
<p>Malone stayed in contact with Newmark despite his hesitation, continuing to ask questions and leaving the door open. The situation shifted at the Final Four, when <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284879/Tommy-Lloyd-Agrees-To-New-Five-Year-Deal-With-Arizona">Arizona's Tommy Lloyd</a> and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284932/Dusty-May-Commits-To-Michigan-Wont-Leave-For-North-Carolina-Job">Michigan's Dusty May</a> both removed themselves from consideration. With their top alternatives gone, Newmark and director of basketball operations Eric Hoots flew from Indianapolis to Malone's home in Colorado on Easter Sunday to make a direct appeal.</p>
<p>Malone's initial reaction to that request was also resistance.</p>
<p>"A lot longer than we wanted him there," Malone joked of the eventual five-hour meeting. "My first reaction when they said they wanted to fly from Indianapolis was like, no ... but I'm so happy that they did come, because it changed my mind and changed my wife's mind - and that night I felt so much better about it."</p>
<p>Additional factors helped close the deal. Malone's daughter Bridget, a member of UNC's volleyball team, told her father directly that she wanted him to take the position. His late father, Brendan, a longtime coach, had been a lifelong admirer of UNC and Dean Smith.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284975/Michael-Malone-Signs-Six-Year-$50M-Deal-With-North-Carolina">Malone signed a six-year, $50 million contract with the Tar Heels</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 08 Apr 2026 22:52:57 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Malone_Michael_den_230109.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Anthony Davis Details Shock Of Learning About Luka Doncic Trade, Says Lakers Showed Him No Respect</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285000/Anthony-Davis-Details-Shock-Of-Learning-About-Luka-Doncic-Trade-Says-Lakers-Showed-Him-No-Respect</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Davis revealed he had no advance knowledge of the trade that sent him from the Los Angeles Lakers to the Dallas Mavericks in exchange for Luka Doncic, describing the night of Feb. 1, 2025 as one of complete disbelief during an appearance on "The Draymond Green Show" released Wednesday.</p>
<p>Davis said his agent, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul, was the first to inform him of the deal. His initial reaction was skepticism.</p>
<p>"I said, who the f- they gonna trade me for? Luka?" Davis said. "I was like, 'Who else could you trade me for on that team?'"</p>
<p>While still on the phone with Paul, then-Mavericks general manager Nico Harrison called Davis directly.</p>
<p>"I told you I was going to come get you one day. I wanted you," Harrison told him, according to Davis. Davis replied: "Huh?"</p>
<p>Dallas sent Doncic to the Lakers for Davis, Max Christie and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/detailed">a 2029 unprotected first-round pick in the deal</a>, which came just eight months after the Mavericks reached the NBA Finals.</p>
<p>"I had no idea this was happening," Davis said. "And I couldn't make sense of it. From a business standpoint, I can't be mad. But why? It just caught me off guard."</p>
<p>Davis made his Mavericks debut on Feb. 8, 2025, suffering a left adductor strain in his first game. He dealt with four separate injuries during his year in Dallas before being traded to the Washington Wizards in February. He has appeared in just 20 games this season and has been sidelined since January with a left hand injury.</p>
<p>Davis, who won a championship alongside LeBron James in Los Angeles in 2020, expressed frustration over how his departure was handled after nearly six seasons with the franchise.</p>
<p>"I still think I deserved much more respect than that," Davis said. "All the time I've been here. S-, I was there six years. We won a championship. All this stuff, and I can't get a phone call or a text of, 'This is what we're thinking.'"</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 08 Apr 2026 20:57:34 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285000/Anthony-Davis-Details-Shock-Of-Learning-About-Luka-Doncic-Trade-Says-Lakers-Showed-Him-No-Respect</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Davis_Anthony_dal_260109.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Investigating Kings' Foul On Seth Curry, Sources Say It Was Coaching Mistake, Not Tanking</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284999/NBA-Investigating-Kings-Foul-On-Seth-Curry-Sources-Say-It-Was-Coaching-Mistake-Not-Tanking</link>
      <description><p>The NBA is reviewing a late-game decision by Sacramento Kings coach Doug Christie during Tuesday's 110-105 loss to the Golden State Warriors, with Kings sources telling ESPN the move was a tactical miscalculation rather than a deliberate effort to lose.</p>
<p>With Sacramento leading by one and 3:15 remaining, Christie called for forward Doug McDermott to foul Seth Curry despite the Kings being in the penalty. Sources said Christie miscalculated that Golden State was in the bonus, sending Curry, an 86.4% career free throw shooter, to the line when more favorable fouling targets were available. Christie's intent, sources said, was to use a timeout before automatically losing it when the clock dropped below three minutes.</p>
<p>Curry converted one of two free throws, tying the game at 101. Christie then drew up a successful out-of-timeout play for a McDermott three-pointer, putting Sacramento back up by three after the team had trailed by 16. The Kings ultimately surrendered that lead in the closing minutes.</p>
<p>The decision drew pointed public criticism from Warriors forward Draymond Green, who connected it to the league's broader tanking concerns.</p>
<p>"I saw a team tonight foul Seth Curry with three minutes to go for no reason," Green said. "I get fined when I do wrong. Fine the hell out of people."</p>
<p>Sacramento dropped to 21-59 with the loss, tied with the Utah Jazz for the league's fourth-worst record. The Kings have been without Domantas Sabonis, Zach LaVine and De'Andre Hunter due to season-ending surgeries, with DeMar DeRozan and Russell Westbrook also inactive Tuesday.</p>
<p>Despite an organizational awareness that finishing near the bottom improves their lottery position in a strong draft class, team sources were firm that Christie and the players on the floor were competing to win.</p>
<p>"Tanking is the last thing I'd do," Christie said after a recent win. "I respect the game too much."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 08 Apr 2026 20:52:50 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284999/NBA-Investigating-Kings-Foul-On-Seth-Curry-Sources-Say-It-Was-Coaching-Mistake-Not-Tanking</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Christie_Doug_sac_241227.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Executives, Coaches Acknowledge Tanking Widely Disliked But Remains Most Effective Rebuilding Strategy</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284998/NBA-Executives-Coaches-Acknowledge-Tanking-Widely-Disliked-But-Remains-Most-Effective-Rebuilding-Strategy</link>
      <description><p>Despite near-universal criticism from players, coaches and front office personnel, tanking has become the dominant team-building strategy for bottom-tier NBA franchises, with league sources acknowledging it will persist until the league establishes firm rules or meaningful penalties to stop it.</p>
<p>ESPN spoke with dozens of individuals across the league and found broad agreement on the problem, if not the solution. Golden State Warriors coach Steve Kerr offered the most direct assessment.</p>
<p>"I hate it," Kerr said.</p>
<p>The on-court consequences have been stark. The average margin of victory in NBA games this season stands at 13.1 points, the largest in league history. A record 89 games have been decided by 30 or more points.</p>
<p>Yet even those who find the practice troubling acknowledge its effectiveness. One Western Conference general manager detailed the scope of tactics currently in use.</p>
<p>"These teams are doing the whole gamut: sitting guys in the fourth, playing analytically bad lineups, drawing up plays for bad shots," the executive said. "The creativity is impressive and I don't blame them. It's the best strategy to get better. Look at all the most promising teams in the league: Thunder, Spurs, Pistons, Rockets, Hornets. Years of being bad and building up on high picks. It's painful but worthwhile."</p>
<p>The results of successful rebuilds have reinforced the strategy's appeal. The Oklahoma City Thunder, San Antonio Spurs and Detroit Pistons each endured prolonged losing stretches before emerging as legitimate contenders built around high lottery selections.</p>
<p>Sources across the league agree the practice will continue in varying forms across different seasons and draft classes until the NBA establishes either structural rule changes or financial consequences severe enough to alter front office behavior.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284258/Adam-Silver-Pledges-Substantial-Draft-Lottery-Changes-To-Combat-Tanking">Commissioner Adam Silver has pledged a resolution before June</a>, with new incentive structures targeted for implementation ahead of the 2026 draft.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 08 Apr 2026 20:48:20 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284998/NBA-Executives-Coaches-Acknowledge-Tanking-Widely-Disliked-But-Remains-Most-Effective-Rebuilding-Strategy</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kerr_Steve_gsw_240113.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Victor Wembanyama Ruled Out Wednesday With Rib Contusion, Optimism For Friday Return</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284997/Victor-Wembanyama-Ruled-Out-Wednesday-With-Rib-Contusion-Optimism-For-Friday-Return</link>
      <description><p>San Antonio Spurs center Victor Wembanyama and guard Stephon Castle will not play Wednesday against the Portland Trail Blazers, the team announced. Wembanyama is dealing with a rib contusion while Castle is sidelined with right knee soreness.</p>
<p>The absences carry particular weight for Wembanyama, who needs at least 20 minutes in one more game to reach the NBA's required minimum of 65 games for individual award eligibility.</p>
<p>San Antonio has three games remaining, including Wednesday's contest. The Spurs face the Dallas Mavericks on Friday and Denver Nuggets on Sunday, and the team expressed optimism that both players could return for the Friday matchup. Both participated in shootaround Wednesday morning. The Spurs will be off nearly an entire week before <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/nba/playoffs/">the NBA playoffs</a> begin on April 18th.</p>
<p>"I can't tell you too much of how [Wembanyama] looked, but he heals fast," veteran forward Harrison Barnes said.</p>
<p>Wembanyama sustained the rib injury during Monday's 115-102 win over the Philadelphia 76ers, playing just under 16 minutes before exiting. That appearance counted as an official game under NBA guidelines, which permit two exceptions for games played between 15 and 19 minutes and 59 seconds. He finished with 17 points, five rebounds and three blocks in that outing. Castle recorded a triple-double in the same game with 17 points, 13 assists and 10 rebounds.</p>
<p>San Antonio sits at 60-19, having clinched the Southwest Division title and a top-two seed in the Western Conference. The Spurs trail the Oklahoma City Thunder by three games in the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/standings">conference standings</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 08 Apr 2026 16:07:47 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284997/Victor-Wembanyama-Ruled-Out-Wednesday-With-Rib-Contusion-Optimism-For-Friday-Return</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wembanyama_Victor_sas_240516.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Cade Cunningham To Return From Collapsed Lung Wednesday Against Bucks</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284996/Cade-Cunningham-To-Return-From-Collapsed-Lung-Wednesday-Against-Bucks</link>
      <description><p>Detroit Pistons guard Cade Cunningham is expected to play Wednesday against the Milwaukee Bucks, sources told ESPN, marking his return from a collapsed left lung that sidelined him for 11 games.</p>
<p>Cunningham suffered the injury March 17 against the Washington Wizards. Initial assessments suggested he would miss the remainder of the regular season and only ramp up during the week before the playoffs open April 18.</p>
<p>Instead, Cunningham recovered ahead of schedule and is available for Detroit's final three regular-season contests after appearing on the injury report as doubtful in recent days.</p>
<p>In 61 games this season, Cunningham is posting 24.5 points, 9.9 assists, 5.6 rebounds and 1.5 steals per game in 34.4 minutes.</p>
<p>Detroit thrived during his absence, going 8-3 and clinching home-court advantage throughout the Eastern Conference playoffs on Saturday.</p>
<p>His injury has carried implications beyond the court. The National Basketball Players Association is now pushing to eliminate the 65-game minimum requirement for postseason award eligibility, citing Cunningham's situation as a breaking point for a rule the union accepted three years ago.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 08 Apr 2026 13:58:07 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284996/Cade-Cunningham-To-Return-From-Collapsed-Lung-Wednesday-Against-Bucks</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cunningham_Cade_det_250428.jpg" />
   </item>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284995/Jaden-McDaniels-Nearing-Return-From-Left-Knee-Tendinopathy</link>
      <description><p>Jaden McDaniels has been cleared for full-contact, 5-on-5 basketball activities and will be listed as questionable for Wednesday's game against the Orlando Magic. McDaniels has missed the last six games with left knee patella tendinopathy.</p>
<p>McDaniels has appeared in 71 games this season, averaging 14.8 points, 4.2 rebounds and 2.7 assists in 31.9 minutes.</p>
<p>The Wolves have also been without Anthony Edwards for parts of this stretch.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Doc Rivers Hints At Retirement, Cites Grandchildren As Bucks' Disappointing Season Winds Down</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284993/Doc-Rivers-Hints-At-Retirement-Cites-Grandchildren-As-Bucks-Disappointing-Season-Winds-Down</link>
      <description><p>Milwaukee Bucks head coach Doc Rivers indicated Tuesday his time on an NBA sideline may be drawing to a close, citing family priorities as his contract situation and the team's uncertain future hang over the franchise.</p>
<p>Rivers, whose deal runs through next season, made the remarks when asked how long he envisioned continuing to coach.</p>
<p>"I won't answer that, but I have grandkids that I want to see," Rivers told reporters. "I have seven grandkids now, and they're all 8 years and under, and it kills me every time I miss grandparents day with each one of them in school. It's probably time to go see them more, so I'll let you figure out the rest."</p>
<p>It marked the second such comment in a week. Before Milwaukee's March 31 win over Dallas, Rivers reflected on his tenure with a tone that struck reporters as valedictory.</p>
<p>"When I was brought here two years ago, 2 1/2 years ago, I was brought into here to take the team to the next level and that just never happened, never materialized," Rivers said. "From a coaching perspective, you feel like the city that you're from, you didn't get the job done, and that is something I carry very heavy with me."</p>
<p>Rivers took over mid-season in 2023-24, replacing Adrian Griffin. Milwaukee has posted a sub-.500 record since and will miss the playoffs for the first time since 2016.</p>
<p>Despite the struggles, GM Jon Horst praised Rivers' work during a difficult year.</p>
<p>"I do know it was an incredibly tough year and, in my opinion, Doc did his absolute best to keep the train on the rails as long as possible and I think it was kind of masterful in a lot of ways," Horst said. "He was unbelievable to work with throughout the year with it."</p>
<p>Rivers has accumulated the sixth-most regular-season victories in NBA coaching history across 27 seasons, including a championship with Boston in 2008. Rivers was recently voted into the Hall of Fame.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 08 Apr 2026 11:29:03 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284993/Doc-Rivers-Hints-At-Retirement-Cites-Grandchildren-As-Bucks-Disappointing-Season-Winds-Down</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Rivers_Doc_mil_240226.jpg" />
   </item>
